title: Weathering the Storm
concept: Weather elements like rain, snow, fog, and lens smudges distort and occlude light, confusing pixel-level features.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main visual effect of overexposure?

options.text: Pixels clip to maximum brightness (255), destroying texture details in white glare
The image color channels are inverted to grayscale
The bounding box coordinates are shifted by 10 pixels

exercises.explain: Overexposure saturates pixels, clipping them to maximum limits and wiping out surface textures.

cards.title: Through the Fog
cards.body: When you look through a foggy window, the details of the street blend into a grey haze. Light scatters as it hits water droplets in the air. For a vision model, this scattering destroys the sharp contrast edges it needs to detect shapes.

cards.title: Weather Distortion
cards.body: Weather Distortion refers to atmospheric phenomena—such as rain (streaks, droplet occlusions), snow (white patches, background clutter), and fog (contrast reduction due to light scattering)—that degrade visual details.

cards.title: A Smudged Window
cards.body: Imagine trying to read a book through a window covered in water droplets and mud smudges. Some words are distorted by water curves (refraction), some are completely blocked, and the overall contrast is muddy. Weather acts like that smudged window on a camera.

cards.title: Fog Contrast Drop
cards.body: In dense fog, light scattering causes a severe drop in contrast. The difference between a dark car and the grey road shrinks to a tiny margin. A model trained on high-contrast sunny days fails because its edge filters detect nothing.

cards.title: Rain is not just streaks
cards.body: You might think rain is easy to model as simple vertical lines overlayed on images. In reality, rain creates water pools on the ground (causing reflections), splashes, and drops on the camera lens that act as distorting glass lenses.

cards.title: Atmospheric Distortions
cards.body: Comparing different weather effects on pixels:

left.text:
Scatters light uniformly.
Reduces global image contrast.
Fades distant objects to grey.

right.text:
Adds random white noise blobs.
Blocks parts of objects (occlusion).
Creates complex, noisy backgrounds.

exercises.prompt: How does fog primarily degrade vision model performance?
exercises.explain: Fog scatters light rays, blending object colors with the background grey and smoothing out edge features.

options.text: It scatters light, severely reducing contrast and wiping out sharp edge gradients
It slows down the execution speed of the convolutional layers
It changes the image files from RGB to binary arrays
It deletes the bounding box coordinates from the annotations

exercises.prompt: True or False: Droplets directly on the camera lens act like tiny lenses, refracting light and distorting local parts of the image.
exercises.explain: True. Water drops on the lens distort light pathing, creating localized blur and refraction spots.
Snowflakes block parts of objects, creating occlusion that hides key identifier features.
exercises.sentence: Falling snow creates random white patches that cause object ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a visual effect of rain in outdoor vision systems?
exercises.explain: Rain introduces multiple visual changes: streaks, droplets, splatters, and reflective ground surfaces.

options.text: Visual streaks, reflections on wet asphalt, and water droplets on the lens
An automatic increase in the camera's resolution
A reduction in the model's weight parameter count

exercises.prompt: A self-driving delivery robot fails to detect pedestrians when it rains because of water droplets clinging to its camera lens. What is the best hardware-software hybrid fix?

options.text: Install a physical lens shield or hydrophobic coating (hardware), and train the model with droplet distortion augmentations (software).
Delete all camera inputs and guide the robot using GPS coordinates only.
Increase the training learning rate to evaporate the virtual water.
Replace the camera lens with a paper screen.

exercises.explain: Hydrophobic coating physically repels water, while droplet augmentations train the model to handle any remaining visual blur.
